notes: New shieldbreak. 27 primary-research studies screened from 1178 raw PubMed hits across 12 intervention-family queries. 40 rows generated at (study x tissue x timepoint-cluster) granularity. Many numeric Treg PD values live in figures/supplement and are recorded as null with notes — follow-up full-text extraction pass warranted. 6 reviews captured in reviews.jsonl side-list. Notable surfaced conflict: Attia 2005 (DD failed in melanoma) vs Dannull 2005 (DD depleted Tregs in RCC) — both retained. Rendered against styles/treg-depletion/STYLE.md.

Backfill pass over 40 rows from the initial 2026-04-23 run. Fetched full text via PMC E-utilities for 28 rows (PMCID-bearing + 6 newly discovered via Europe PMC search); 10 rows remain abstract-only because journals are not in Europe PMC OA (Int J Cancer, Clin Exp Immunol, J Immunother, Clin Cancer Res subscription, Br J Dermatol, Inflamm Bowel Dis, Cancer Discov). Backfilled numeric baseline+post values on 28/38 rows (74%). EXCLUDED 2 rows to excluded.jsonl: Telang 2011 (PMID 22165955) — Phase II DD trial but no Treg measurement performed in this cohort, title is misleading; Galsky 2025 PORTER (PMID 39964352) — nivolumab combination platform, Tregs not targeted or pre-specified. Reclassified Liao 2024 ascites row from 'succeeded' to 'partial' (p=0.2737 n.s. with small n=3). Hamid 2011 intent reclassified from 'exploratory-but-prespecified' to 'incidental-but-measured' — baseline-only biomarker study, not depletion endpoint. New PMCIDs added for Chand 2024 (PMC11609826), Gogas 2024 (PMC11258232), Vaishampayan 2024 (PMC11580269), Calvo 2025 (PMC12323519), Piha-Paul 2025 (PMC12574322). New conflict pairs surfaced / sharpened: (a) Ager 2026 (Fc-enhanced anti-CTLA-4 depletes TI-Tregs, p=0.031) vs Sharma 2019 (non-Fc-enhanced anti-CTLA-4 fails to deplete) — Fc engineering is the reconciling variable; (b) Huang 2011 tremelimumab INCREASED intratumoral FOXP3+ cells 35→167 cells/mm^2 (p=0.0029) corroborates Sharma 2019's broader null; (c) Gogas 2024 PIVOT-02 shows 8-10x Treg absolute expansion with bempeg outpacing CD8 2x expansion — the bempeg ratio-shift path is actually unfavorable in this dataset. Honest caveat: older denileukin-diftitox papers (Audia 2007, Atchison 2010, Mahnke 2007) and older CTLA-4 papers (Ribas 2009, Nancey 2012) are in non-OA journals and could not be backfilled beyond abstract values.

Scope expanded from Treg-depletion-only to depletion OR inhibition OR destabilization. Searched 11 new intervention-family queries (anti-TNFR2, anti-TIGIT, EP4 antagonists, DNMTi/HMA Treg-focused, HDACi Treg-focused, Helios/IKZF2/CELMoDs, PI3Kδ function-focused, NRP1, anti-GITR/OX40 function-focused, FoxP3 destabilization, iberdomide follow-up); 306 total hits, 285 unique new candidates after dedup vs existing 38 rows; 13 kept after screening. Honest literature characterization: (1) anti-TNFR2 clinical human PD literature does not exist in PubMed as of 2026-04-23 — Torrey 2019 Sézary paper is ex-vivo only; BAT2506/HFB200301 have no published Treg PD. (2) Helios/IKZF2-selective degraders are preclinical only. (3) CELMoDs (iberdomide, mezigdomide) degrade Ikaros/Aiolos, NOT IKZF2/Helios — so they INCREASE rather than destabilize Tregs (Lipsky 2022 SLE +104.9% Tregs at 0.45 mg, p<0.001). (4) DNMT inhibitors (decitabine, azacitidine) INCREASE Treg quantity and function in most clinical contexts (Han 2021 ITP; Penter 2023 post-ipilimumab MDS/AML) — opposite to hypothesis; Govindaraj 2014 aza+panobinostat is the one exception targeting TNFR2+ Treg subset. (5) HDACi effects are heterogeneous and context-dependent: entinostat and vorinostat DECREASE intratumoral Tregs in oncology (Pili 2017 RCC; Terranova-Barberio 2020 breast, 11.8%→2.9% p=0.0067); panobinostat INCREASES Tregs in HIV cART setting (Brinkmann 2018, +40% p=0.003). (6) Anti-TIGIT clinical Treg depletion demonstrated only in PBMC (Guan 2024 Nature CITYSCAPE/GO30103 translational n=16), not in tumor. (7) Idelalisib uniformly decreases PBMC Tregs in CLL (Gadi 2022 n=9). New conflicts: Penter 2023 shows ipilimumab EXPANDED (not depleted) marrow Tregs in AML/MDS — reinforces Fc-engineering-reconciling-variable framework from prior run. 0 functional-impairment-only rows added — no clinical paper in this scope expansion reported a suppression assay without an accompanying count/frequency change. Coverage gaps (searched but no ≥3-patient human data): anti-TNFR2, IKZF2-selective degraders, EP4 antagonists, NRP1 blockade, CCR8-CAR-T.

First-time critique pass over all 44 unique papers in treg-depletion shieldbreak. 34 full-text-accessible (28 PMC XML + 6 PMC-paywalled recovered via WebFetched PMC HTML); 10 PubMed-abstract-only (paywalled journals). Zero retractions/corrections/expressions of concern found. One extraction-fidelity discrepancy flagged (Huang 2011 — Mann-Whitney test label applied to paired pre/post biopsy data; screener correctly noted). Per-paper RoB: 3 Low / 4 Some concerns / 32 Moderate / 4 Serious / 1 not amenable (Nancey 2012 case report). Per-trial confidence: 2 High / 23 Moderate / 18 Low / 1 Very low. Cross-paper synthesis added to schema.json (critique_synthesis and critique_topline_override fields). Key findings: (1) CD25-gating confound in DD studies is pervasive and partially discounts the Dannull-family positive literature; Attia 2005 and Liao 2024 sidestep via FoxP3 mRNA. (2) Standard anti-CTLA-4 (ipi, treme) does NOT deplete intratumoral Tregs in humans (Sharma 2019, Huang 2011, Penter 2023, Ribas 2009, Comin-Anduix 2008 all converge). (3) Fc-enhanced anti-CTLA-4 (Ager 2026, Chand 2024) DOES deplete tumor Tregs — Fc engineering is the reconciling variable. (4) Bempeg and non-α IL-2 variants expand Tregs absolutely with ratio-shift framing; phase 3 PIVOT-IO failed (2022), so the ratio-shift mechanism did not translate to clinical benefit. (5) CELMoDs (iberdomide Lipsky 2022) and DNMTi (Han 2021, Penter 2023) counterexamples correctly captured by screener — these INCREASE Tregs. (6) Mogamulizumab depletes CCR4+ eTregs rigorously across compartments (Fujikawa 2023, Jinushi 2025, Roelens 2022). (7) Cyclophosphamide Treg-depleting activity is SCHEDULE-dependent (metronomic yes, single-IV no). (8) Compartment dissociation is pervasive — tumor Treg ↓ with tdLN Treg ↑ in Ager 2026 is emblematic. No files outside treg-depletion directories modified except scripts/build_critique.py (new) and docs/runs.md. Not pushed — awaiting user confirmation.

First prescriber pass, 6 ranked interventions (anti-CCR4 > Fc-enhanced anti-CTLA-4 > metronomic cyclophosphamide > DD > HDACi > standard anti-CTLA-4). Grouping PI-approved prior to run. Unranked counterexamples (non-alpha IL-2 variants, anti-CD25 daclizumab, CELMoDs/iberdomide, DNMTi monotherapy, anti-GITR, anti-TIGIT, PI3Kdelta-i) called out in a single paragraph. Cross-cutting caveat: Treg depletion magnitude frequently uncoupled from clinical benefit; CD25-gating confound pervasive across DD and anti-CD25 literature; compartment dissociation is common. Extended scripts/build_table.py to inline docs/shieldbreaks/<slug>/scope_summary.md under ## Scope summary above the trial table; default class/tissue bullets moved to a 'Scope inventory' subsection. mkdocs build --strict passed. Not pushed — awaiting user confirmation.

Targeted schema-extension backfill for three new fields introduced in .claude/agents/trialist_skeptic.md on 2026-04-23: counter_productive_mechanisms (narrative), counter_productive_tags (list of short tags), counter_productive_severity (Low/Moderate/High/Unknown). All 44 existing critiques superseded by appended v2 rows with supersedes pointing back to prior critique_id; all other narrative/RoB/confidence/bias_flags fields preserved verbatim (no re-appraisal of the other 10 dimensions). Severity distribution: 15 High, 19 Moderate, 10 Low, 0 Unknown. High-severity cluster (15): CELMoD iberdomide Amatangelo 2024 + Lipsky 2022 SLE (opposite-direction mechanism, spare Helios/IKZF2); DNMTi monotherapy Han 2021 ITP + Penter 2023 decitabine+ipi AML (TSDR demethylation induces Tregs); panobinostat Brinkmann 2018 HIV (+40% Tregs); non-alpha IL-2 variants Bentebibel 2019 + Diab 2020 + Gogas 2024 + Calvo 2025 + Vaishampayan 2024 + Piha-Paul 2025 (treg-expansion-by-design + denominator-artifact-framing, bempeg PIVOT-IO phase 3 failure as clinical evidence); daclizumab Mahnke 2007 + Morse 2008 (IL-2Ra blockade on CD8 effectors); mogamulizumab Fujikawa 2023 + Jinushi 2025 (paper-internal CCR4+ central memory CD8 collateral). Moderate-severity cluster (19): mostly denileukin diftitox (CD25+ effector collateral) and Fc-enhanced anti-CTLA-4/anti-GITR/anti-TIGIT (ADCC on activated effectors). Low-severity cluster (10): standard anti-CTLA-4 (drug doesn't deplete so collateral is moot), metronomic cyclo, class-I-selective HDACi, IP denileukin diftitox. Added Counter-productive-mechanism severity distribution table to critique.md summary stats; added Counter-productive-mechanism patterns across interventions bullet block to schema.json critique_synthesis. No changes to other files outside treg-depletion directories except scripts/build_critique.py (new dimension rendering) and scripts/backfill_counterproductive.py (new one-shot). mkdocs build --strict passes clean. Not pushed; awaiting user confirmation.
CP-MoA subsection + Counter-productive MoA column + 4 new caveats bullets; no ranking reshuffle; aggregation rule = modal + bump-one-on-replicated-paper-internal-High-or-wrong-direction

New shieldbreak. 12 intervention-family PubMed queries (CSF1R, PI3Kg, CCR2/5, bisphosphonate, trabectedin/lurbinectedin, CD47/SIRPa, CD24, CD40, TLR, STING, CLEVER-1/MARCO, LILRB/TREM2), plus narrowed clinical-agent-token queries where LILRB/TREM2 (1630->43) and CLEVER-1/MARCO (351->39) needed scope tightening. ~2010 total raw hits, 30 papers kept after title/abstract screening at the 30-cap, 37 rows produced (row-grain: some papers yield multiple tissue/cohort rows, e.g., Razak 2020 AMG820+pembro produced 2 rows for PBMC monocytes + tumor biopsy immune infiltrate; Yeku 2025 produced 2 rows for parallel PY159 and PY314 arms). All rows abstract-derived (source tier pubmed_abstract); no full-text fetch on this pass. Coverage: CSF1R family is deepest (13 rows across 11 papers including emactuzumab TGCT + ICB combos, pexidartinib mono and combos, LY3022855, AMG 820, axatilimab cGVHD, cabiralizumab combo). CD47: 4 rows (Advani NEJM, Sallman MDS, Daver AML, Strati NHL). CD40: 4 rows (Byrne PDAC, Soto E/GEJ, Coveler SEA-CD40, Weiss multi-agent). CLEVER-1: 3 (Rannikko MATINS, Virtakoivu, Kontro aza). LILRB/TREM2: 4 (Taylor IO-108, Yeku PY159+PY314, Beckermann PY314+pembro). CCR2/5: 3 (Nywening, Noel, Grierson). PI3Kg: 2 (Hong MARIO-1, O'Connell MARIO-3). Trabectedin: 2 (Germano, Sun TARMIC). STING: 1 (Meric-Bernstam ADU-S100). TLR: 1 (Bhatia G100). Coverage gaps with no in-scope clinical TAM PD paper found: bisphosphonates (none met intent-to-modulate-TAM gate despite search), anti-CD24 (IMM47 preclinical only), anti-CD40 mitazalimab pure TAM-PD paper (Moreno/Van Laethem lack direct TAM PD in abstract), most CD40 agonist pancreatic papers (clinical-efficacy focused; TAM PD not in abstract). Exclusion list has 24 entries — preclinical papers, CPI-monotherapy, healthy-volunteer PD, review articles, and clinically positive papers with no direct TAM PD readout. 7 reviews side-listed. Extended scripts/build_table.py with: (1) `repolarization` and `expansion-with-repolarization` change_mechanism chips (new `mech-neutral` CSS class, purple); (2) TAM gating_quality pill mapping for CD68-CD163/CD14-CD16/mIF-multiplex/CyTOF/scRNA/single-marker-only/mixed-or-unclear; (3) scRNA-cluster-shift + phagocytosis-function readout chips; (4) classify_success override so repolarization `succeeded` renders as 'repolarized' green pill instead of 'increase' orange; (5) n_treated field fallback from _treg_ to _tam_ variant. mkdocs build --strict passes. Not pushed; awaiting user confirmation.

First-pass critique of all 35 unique PMIDs across 37 trial rows. Full 11-dimension appraisal with counter_productive_mechanisms / counter_productive_tags / counter_productive_severity populated for every paper on first pass per user instruction (no separate backfill). CP severity distribution: 6 High, 25 Moderate, 4 Unknown, 0 Low. 4 extraction discrepancies flagged in Manji 2021 (PMID 34321280): n_treated_with_tam_measurement 18 vs ~3 paired qmIF patients; gating_quality CD68-CD163-based vs 6-marker mIF-multiplex; change_mechanism absolute-reduction vs proportion-based repolarization; depletion_success succeeded vs partial-at-best given paper's own 'non-statistically significant trends' language. Flagged in critiques.jsonl + per-paper page; trials.jsonl untouched per skeptic/screener separation. No retractions; phase 3 ENHANCE futility for Sallman 2023 + Daver 2025 flagged as a non-retraction context signal. Scripts change: scripts/build_table.py DIMENSION_SECTIONS entry renamed from ('Treg gating / definition', 'treg_gating_notes') to ('Gating / definition', 'gating_notes') with legacy fallback to treg_gating_notes and tam_gating_notes so existing treg-depletion critiques render unchanged; scripts/build_critique.py gets the same fallback for the master critique report. mkdocs build --strict passes. Not pushed; awaiting user confirmation.

First prescriber pass. 4 ranked (CSF1R > CD40 > CD47/SIRPa > CCR2/5) + 5 unranked (CLEVER-1, LILRB/TREM2, PI3Kg, trabectedin, STING). Zero Low-CP-severity across set; CSF1R CP=High driven by Gomez-Roca paper-internal inverse-correlation finding.
